D7 Print L1 Identify Atom

Print vs Screen

paper reflects whatever light falls on it; a screen makes its own — the two react to a darkening room in opposite ways

A printed page is a reflective object: it has no light of its own, only ink absorbing some of the ambient light bouncing back to your eye. A screen is emissive: it generates the light it sends you. Turn the room lights down and the page disappears while the screen still glows; brighten them up and the page jumps to life while the screen washes out.

Drag the room light up and down and watch the two panels move opposite to one another. This is why colour management on print is anchored to a standard viewing booth, while screen colour is anchored to a fixed emission curve.
